So I've been mulling it over the last couple of weeks, whether I will be adding the Mobridge into the car to get iPod integration, or do a complete head unit replacement, and finally decided on a complete replacement using a Kenwood navigation unit and the AVIN MOST adapter to feed the existing Bose amp.

I already started to gather all the parts in anticipation of this, and I just though of a question:

Once I remove the [Comand](https://www.amazon.com/s?k=Mercedes+COMAND+system&tag=vs-pl-benzworld-org-20&rel=nofollow), will I need to code it out of the system? If I don't, what will happen?

I assume there will be some DTC's somewhere, but will they affect the car functionality in any way?

[#1](https://www.benzworld.org/threads/complete-head-unit-command-replacement.3020600/#post-17728856)· [Mar 17, 2019](https://www.benzworld.org/threads/complete-head-unit-command-replacement.3020600/#post-17728856)

[#2](https://www.benzworld.org/threads/complete-head-unit-command-replacement.3020600/#post-17729248)· [Mar 17, 2019](https://www.benzworld.org/threads/complete-head-unit-command-replacement.3020600/#post-17729248)

You will want to code the instrument cluster to remove [COMAND](https://www.amazon.com/s?k=Mercedes+COMAND+system&tag=vs-pl-benzworld-org-20&rel=nofollow) so that you regain the ability to set the clock through its menus. Also, it will remove the NAV infro screen of the MFD so you don't get "no NAV" messages".

[#3](https://www.benzworld.org/threads/complete-head-unit-command-replacement.3020600/#post-17732952)· [Mar 21, 2019](https://www.benzworld.org/threads/complete-head-unit-command-replacement.3020600/#post-17732952)

For the past few days I've been searching all over for either DIY walk-throughs or videos of someone replacing the [Comand](https://www.amazon.com/s?k=Mercedes+COMAND+system&tag=vs-pl-benzworld-org-20&rel=nofollow) unit in a [SL500](https://www.amazon.com/s?k=SL500&tag=vs-pl-benzworld-org-20&rel=nofollow)/550, and I really can't find anything about how to connect the aftermarket radio.

There are tons of videos on how to get the unit out, but very little information on how to put the new unit in, specifically how to connect it to the existing wiring. The NTG1 unit in the 07 [SL550](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0DNMZCF2G?tag=vs-pl-benzworld-org-20&linkCode=osi&th=1&psc=1&rel=nofollow) has only 1 wiring harness, with the fiberoptic connector, and 3 other wires. There are no antenna connectors or anything else for that matter.

Now, the Kenwood HU has the typical connections, and I can probably find my way into making the unit functional, but there are a few things I can't find, and I'm wondering if anyone here can help:

1\. Where is the radio antenna located?

2\. Where can I find 12V Continuous (This actually is not that big a thing, I'm sure I can find it if I put my mind to it)

3\. Where can I find the Illumination dimmer

4\. Where is the CAN BUS-B, and which wires do I tap into in order to keep the steering wheel controls.

I plan on using the GPS antenna puck that came with the Kenwood, and putting it under the center speaker cover. I also plan on using the Avin/Xtrons fiberoptic MOST adapter, so I will be using the pre-amp outputs from the new unit. The output is 2 channel, but from what I understand, the BOSE Amp will automatically fill in the sound to the center, Sub and rear speakers.

@ Rudeney

Regarding the center console clock, on my 07, I have the option sync the clock to the head unit, or not. If I disable that, I can set the clock manually from the steering wheel menus.

So any help locating those items and how to best get to them would be greatly appreciated.

[#4](https://www.benzworld.org/threads/complete-head-unit-command-replacement.3020600/#post-17732990)· [Mar 22, 2019](https://www.benzworld.org/threads/complete-head-unit-command-replacement.3020600/#post-17732990)

I found some more information right after I posted the questions above.

It looks like the antennas (2 of them) are connected to the Audio gateway, which is located under the side paneling behind the driver. Also the at the gateway, right next to the MOST connector are the inputs (outputs) for the CAN-BUS Hi and Low wires.

So the question regarding the antennas is, do I simply unplug it from the AGW and plug it into the new head unit (with an adapter and extension of course), If so, which one do you use? is this an antenna diversity setup the [R230](https://www.amazon.com/s?k=r230&tag=vs-pl-benzworld-org-20&rel=nofollow) uses?

BY the same token I have to figure out the can bus on my Kenwood. I have a decoder, that came with my Connects 2 installation kit, but it has a lot of wires and no instructions that I can find (yet)

As I had mentioned a couple of posts above, you can tap the antenna as well as the CAN-BUS directly at the AWG, which is located behind the paneling behind the driver.

Here is a photo I found showing the AWG location. Ignore all the other stuff this user is trying to install a DVD player, but the big silver box with 2 antennas coming into it is the AWG. The CAN-BUS wires are n the same connector that has the Orange MOST cables.

[#8](https://www.benzworld.org/threads/complete-head-unit-command-replacement.3020600/#post-17811714)· [Jun 11, 2019\\
\\
\\
(Edited)](https://www.benzworld.org/threads/complete-head-unit-command-replacement.3020600/#post-17811714)

You can get some extra information on my refit on this thread:

[https://www.benzworld.org/forums/r230-sl-class/3027168-command-replacement.html](https://www.benzworld.org/forums/r230-sl-class/3027168-command-replacement.html)

_Did you find out which one of the 2 Fakra connectors is for the radio?_

I assume you're asking about the antennas. If so, the black antenna is the one I used and works just fine.

_Lastly did you find a permanent 12v supply for your Kenwood?_

Yes, the 12V continuous can be found on the cable that plugs into the back of the Comand. There are 3 wires, one is 12V cont., the other ground, and the 3rd is wake-up. (I'm not home right now, so I can't tel you which, but I'll add the info later this evening). I tapped the 12V cont only. The 12V switched and the ground I tapped at the cigarette lighter.

Regarding the photo above. That was just a photo I found on line that showed were the ADW was located. it's not my actual setup.

FWIW, while I didn't have the Dension, I did have the Mobridge ABT2010 installed previously, and it works well, especially the Bluetooth phone integration and retention of all the steering wheel control and in-dash MFC, but my problem had always been the iPod support. It does work, and you have access to your iPod music, but song or Artist navigation is terrible. I had found that the only way to get around it was to either play your music randomly, or to create specific playlists.

With the Kenwood, I have an awesome head unit, and with the Avin adapter I have the great sound of the original BOSE, but I'm having no luck getting the steering wheel controls to work. I don't have the Axxess adapter you have, I have the Connects 2, and unfortunately none of the controls work. Connects support is pretty much useless, as their entire testing and support is predicated on the car with Audio20 and no Bose amp. So maybe you'll have better luck with your interface. Let us know.

One question: on your diagram, you show the left channel of the Avin interface plugged into the Left Front of the Kenwood, and the right plugged into the rear Right. Any particular reason you did that?

**Update:**

I took a look at my notes, and the 12V constant power is on the Red/Yellow wire on the NTG1 Comand radio connector. Pull that from the back of the radio, tap into that red/yellow wire for the 12V, then remove the MOST connector and plug it into the AVIN adapter.

Hey Dudeman - nice install. Question about the GPS and sat radio pucks. Is there any problem with reception with those under the speaker cover? Every time I want to add [Sirius](https://www.amazon.com/s?k=Sirius&tag=vs-pl-benzworld-org-20&rel=nofollow), I am told the puck should be mounted externally on the vehicle - and I don't want to do that. [#10](https://www.benzworld.org/threads/complete-head-unit-command-replacement.3020600/#post-17812802)· [Jun 12, 2019](https://www.benzworld.org/threads/complete-head-unit-command-replacement.3020600/#post-17812802)

Yeah, there might be something to that. My [Sirius](https://www.amazon.com/s?k=Sirius&tag=vs-pl-benzworld-org-20&rel=nofollow) XM reception leaves something to be desired. when I drive around the city (San Francisco, there are entire blocks that the reception is either totally gone, or frizzy (lot's of drop outs). On the open road it's OK, but even then, there are some areas that I get drop outs.

At the moment, I'm using the 3 month free trial, so I'm not sure if I plan on keeping it, but if I do, I'll probably end up moving the puck to the outside, or figure out how to tap the one that was already on the car.

Some people installed the puck inside the driver side Hood vent, then routed the cable through the firewall in the same place that the wire harness comes through (behind the driver side engine bay fusebox).

[![](https://www.metrisforum.com/d1/avatars/m/20/20667.jpg?1602348763)](https://www.metrisforum.com/members/serge9898.20667/)

**“Metris Stock Audio 15 Radio HU replacement for Dummies”**

I installed a new **Head unit (HU): BOSS Audio BE920WCPA**(link) $229 HU. Total spent ~$400

**\* See my update after 6 months of use at the end of this article \***

I wanted to share my experience and wrote this with **beginners like me** in mind, who have limited interest and knowledge like me. I leveraged other excellent posts. (Special thanks to River’s 2021 and Thomas’ 2024 posts..)

Abbreviations used are listed at the end.

**My needs (facing the huge offer I first listed my needs, in this order):**

Improve audio quality\*, highest HU power without an external amplifier, cheap/best deal, Car Play/Android, keep all SW (Steering Wheel) controls and BU camera. One stop shopping and getting good support/help and easy return if needed. Good warranty.

Plus **optionally**: keep the “Audio/Phone information” in the Driver Info Display of the dashboard, play CDs, analog volume control.

I selected the Boss Audio after reading many reviews and this Crutchfield award [Best car stereos for 2024](https://www.crutchfield.com/learn/best-car-stereo.html) for _“Wireless Android Auto and Apple CarPlay on a budget”_ and its power 20w RMS. (and an unrealistic 85w peak..) and a 5-year warranty, to mitigate the _weak_ brand name. (The highest non amplified HU power below $750 was 22w/channel)

**Note for the beginners like me. You will need 6 items:**

- The Head Unit (HU). (Aka the car stereo, CarPlay/Android receivers, Digital Media video receiver…)
- A dash kit (The set of plastic parts to attach the HU and fill the gaps created by the custom MB HU).
- A wiring interface (a small box and lots of cable and cable converter between the new HU and Metris cables)
- An antenna adapter (with an amplified antenna)
- Some plastic (nylon) pry tool to remove the dash assembly without damaging the clips
- The Crutchfield $24 “Harness Ready” option: They connect/solder all the cables in one clean harness.

This was the Crutchfield’s advised shopping list. (And the known issues)

- Scosche CR1292B Dash Kit. Plastic bezels. [Here](https://www.crutchfield.com/p_142CR1292B/Scosche-CR1292B-Dash-Kit-Black.html). $12\. (Alignment issues according to posts?)
- Metra 40-EU10 Antenna Adapter. [Here](https://www.crutchfield.com/p_12040EU10/Metra-40-EU10-Antenna-Adapter.html). $20 (Does not work on Metris. requires a powered antenna feed to energize the RF amplifier which is why the Metra 40-EU10 doesn't work.
- Crux SWRBM-57 Wiring Interface/ Harness. [Here.](https://www.crutchfield.com/p_249SWRBM57/Crux-SWRBM-57-Wiring-Interface.html) $129 (Does not support BU camera!)


They want to sell a $20 BU cam accessory with a long cable to run. Not an option in my converted van.

After my research to keep BU Cam and the antenna, this is my HW shopping list:

**This is the HW I used for my Metris HU upgrade:**(Minus a $50 discount out of the CF order)

- Metra 99-6512 Single DIN / Double DIN Installation Dash Kit. Better alignment? $16.99 Amazon
- Metra 40-EU55 Antenna Adapter (antenna) $13.39 Amazon free shipping. $19.99 Crutchfield
- PAC RP4.2-MB11 Wiring Interface, ( _Camera features maintained: reverse lines moving with steering_...) $145 Crutchfield

_Connect a new stereo and retain steering wheel audio controls, USB port, aux in, and backup camera in select 2014-20 Mercedes-Benz Sprinters_
- 3 Bojo Trim Pry tools $11.99

**Note about: One stop shopping and technical support:**

Crutchfield service is (mostly) outstanding. (Incl. lifetime technical support) but they are admitting that “they still have not gone through a Metris vehicle to spec what works and what doesn't”. From many articles, Audio 15 head units are being replaced on other MB models successfully and integrated using the PAC adapter listed above.

The CF Sales adviser said he provided the list to their research team, and they hopefully will update their recommended shopping list. However they verified and do support this shopping list they did not recommend in the first place and provide help.

**The replacement process:**

**Prying the “vent center panel”**: refer to several YouTube video (and Crutchfield instructions) that shows how to pry the central vent console. It’s not difficult but I had a hard time locating the clips and didn’t want to pull hard. See my picture with blue tape on the clip locations. Pull gently after inserting the pry tool. Easy 2/5

**Disconnect the HU connectors**. Easy 1/5. Note: to rotate the main connector locking lever, there are 2 clips to press down (picture). (2/5)

I do recommend marking the 3 connectors (USB, BU Cam, Antenna) once unplugged from Audio 15 to ease later re-assembly, as original color coding is lost… (No connector for the BT mic.. not in use)

**Dash kit.**

Mounting the Metra 99-6512 dash kit is easy (1/5). But if the kit aligns well with the “vent center panel”, the alignment with the HU itself does not (a 2mm - 1/8” gap) and some sharp edge trimming needed to fit… (3/5)

I do not recommend this kit for this specific HU. I suggest to go with the Crutchfield recommended one.

**Reconnect the HU connectors.** Easy 1/5. Follows the clear instructions sent by CF. You basically have to connect the harness they sent (with PAC in the middle) between the HU and the MB car big connector. Then the 4 connectors with provide dongles (Antenna, BU Cam, USB)

**CarPlay and USB.**

There are 2 USB port cables on the back of HU. Long enough get them in a convenient location.

One for CarPlay/Android + charging that I plugged in the original USB (adapter supplied with the kit).

The other one to connect a USB drive + charging. I cut a rectangular opening (18 x 10mm) next to other USB port. Secure with a tie wrap. Easy.

**Mic.**

The PAC interface does not support the MB stock BT mic. You need to use the provide one. (Jack 3.5 mm). I drilled a hole next to the USB port (because I didn’t want to disassemble the pillar to run a cable). Simple. Works perfectly. (picture)

**Results and conclusion:**

**Sound quality** is good. This HU provides a significant improvement and meets my project goal.

I replaced the door speakers’ woofers (and dash tweeters) with **Rockford Fosgate R165-S**($75).

Not happy with the result. I do not recommend doing this. (Refer to the 2nd article at the end for details)

**BU Cam.**

Easy to connect. Cable comes in a bag. Works perfectly. Great improvement from stock cam image quality. Requires to be in reverse gear. There is a way to turn it on from the HU “Radio Screen” while driving, which is great to check trailer, bike rack, load rack…

You will get both the “bendy lines” from the stock camera and the HU. You just need to disable the HU lines in the BU setting menu.

**HU User interface** is nice and easy to use (songs and radio information on the HU screen). All SW commands work.

**The drawbacks/lost features:**

- I miss the text for phone and audio information inside the driver info display. Both now show "----" but all other features retained. Information displayed nicely on HU screen. Including the 1000s of song on my USB key, with tiles, album cove pictures. So a none issue for me.


Root cause: The PAC role is to communicate from car to HC, but not from HU to the car. No solution.
- Lost use of the factory BT mic. None issue for me: the provided mic works great and is easy to install.
- Listen to radio with the car key out is not possible. Bummer! (Now installing a Power Delay Switch.$15)
- The slow (14 sec!) boot up and a splash screen that requires a acknowledgement of safety instruction is super annoying. BTW this makes the BU Camera useless for me because I don’t 15 sec idle…

Fix 1: Turn off EULA in the setting mebu to remove the warning splash scree. Save 2 sec.


Fix 2: I am installing a Power Delay Switch. $15 to leave the HU powered like the Audio 15 was (you choose for how long between 1s to 999 min). then it’s instant on
- The volume knob is missing indeed. Not an issue in everyday use. Just need getting used to the convenient SW control. Including instant mute. (It’s only when you want to quickly pop up your favorite song volume..!)

**The benefits/ New features:**

- CarPlay setup is quick & super easy. Just connect the phone once in the USB port, answer 2 pairing popup questions, done. Allow to use Google Map. Split screen feature (Audio + map) is cool.
- Radio user interface is much easier than Audio 15 (Access to USB and SD car, apps..)
- DVD watching (from USB drive or AV in) Not that I will use it, but cute and good quality
- Sound quality is significant improved and exactly what I expected. (13 band equalizer and booster help)
- Assembly was easier than expected. Everything works first time. ½ day job for a slow worker.

**\*Sound quality:** For me: allowing to listen at 50% of max volume with a rich sound and low distortion.

I found very little reviews about the difference in sound distortion between brands and units. And I found couple of articles saying why there is little difference between brands on this criteria for the ~20ish Watt HU (w/o external amplifier)… because they are all using the same chips… “ _the 4 channel audio amplifier chips in all head units are mostly made by ST or Hitachi. The ST chip is slightly cleaner, but it is not a significant difference. Most of the brands use both chips, so you can’t really say brand X is cleaner than brand Y”_

**Abbreviations used:** HU Head unit, MB Mercedes Benz, CarPlay Apple CarPlay, Android for Android Audio, SW Steering Wheel, 1/5 is the difficulty rating out of 5, WI wiring interface/PAC 4.2, BT Bluetooth, CF Crutchfield, Cam camera, BU Cam Backup Camera, Amp amplifier, Metris Vito,

**Issues, troubleshooting and fixes:**

I encounter couple of issues on the way.

USB thumb drive not recognized. The USB drives need to be formatted as FAT32. (Ex FAT format is not read)

Radio does not work with key out of ignition. Lost the RAP (Retain Accessory Power). MB vehicle provides RAP that keeps voltage applied to certain vehicle systems for a given time after the ignition is off and while all (or driver) doors are closed. According to Crutchfield the PAC retains the RAP. Crutchfield replaced the PAC. Didn't solve it.

The DIP Switch setting (red) on the PAC was different on the replacement unit: From Boss Audio tech support, the setting for Boss Audio is switch #3 on. (Like _other brand_)

**Conclusion:**

Replacing the HU worth it for me. Replacing the speaker without adding an amp doesn’t (for me).

I hope you enjoyed this post!. I was desperately looking for a comprehensive simple affordable solution like this and I hope it will help beginners like me to jump in this intimidating job and provide them an easy kick start. There are plenty of other posts with technical info and expert debates. Intimidating to me. Feel free to send me question in PM. I am sure there are mistakes to correct, but before you jump the gun, will it help a beginner or discourage and intimidate them? 😉(And before you shoot: please remember this is for everyone's enjoyment! 😉)

End of article about the HU replacement

Serge,

2017 Metris commercial with a 2018 camper conversion with a Reimo Pop-top roof. Love it! ;-)

**_**Dec 2nd 2024: Update after 6 months of use**_**

After 6 month of use and couple of road trips: I confirm the slight improvement in sound provided by the HU replacement, specifically at low speed and city drive. But without replacing the stock speakers, the sound lacks bass support to listen to any quality music above 60 mph.

Bottom line, replacing the HU alone doesn't worth the amount of work! (Replacing the speakers and adding an amplifier is a must to me). Just my opinion after a longer use...

\-\-\---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**More information about the speaker’s replacement.**

**I also intended to replace the MB stock speakers to get a significant sound improvement.**

Upon Crutchfield advise I purchased **Rockford Fosgate R165-S speakers.** 6 1/2” ($75) 92dB sensitivity/efficiency for 60-20k Hz. (92 dB is _noise_ generated for 1 W…92 is very good, higher: you lose too much bass)

FYI the **Kicker 46CSC674,** 6 1/2", 100w ($79) are better quality but were on back order for 2 months.

**Result:** the sound is bad and way worse than the stock speakers. The main issue is there is no bass.

Disclaimer: I don’t know anything about car stereo, but I studied acoustic in college and I spent a few hours researching on line and with experts during project.

First you need to choose between Component speakers or wide range speakers:

Component speakers means you have several speakers to cover the auditive spectrum (tweeter, woofer, subwoofer), each covering a range of frequencies. vs a wide range covering the whole spectrum (often with several coaxial speakers inside).

Second, you need enough power to “move” the speaker!

20 to 25W x 4 channels from the HU is **not** enough power and mover the 90ish dB speakers at low frequency, hence the better sound from the MB stock speakers.

(An expert will argue that it’s still "objectively a better sound quality", but who cares if you have no bass to listen? I am not starting an expert technical debate for the sake of it, just looking for a common sense solution for listening enjoyment)

The MB stock paper light speakers actually “simulates” the bass. And if you turn on the “Loudness surround” feature in the Boss HU settings (signal compression) you get very decent bass sound.

The MB speakers also have significant higher diameter (40% more area…). They sound very good until halfway of the volume. (Not super load indeed but plenty enough for me)

The next step for sound improvement is to add an amplifier to move the speakers. There is ample empty space right below the HU, inside the dash (see YouTube video). Cheapest solutions:

- Kenwood: KAC-M1814 $169. 4x 45W. Bridging the 2 channels gives 2x 90 W
- Pioneer 60 W x 2 . $89.

But you will have to run 12V to the amp and speaker wires to the speakers. And that’s beyond my limits!

(and yes that was a frustrating experience that the CF sales person did not tell me this issue when you purchase. The tech support guy knew it and they "have the same training")

Last, **prying the “vent center panel” for the HU was very easy in comparison of prying the door panels.**

If you attempt it with no experience: watch a few videos first.

I found the prying and putting back the door panels difficult (4/5).

One issue is that some of the 9 orange clips popped from the door panel rather that popping out of the door.

Another is none of the 3 bottom Philips screws came out (they are plastic screws). So you break the plastic snaps when prying. (see pictures)

(Some people would say “who cares!?” My experience with damaging and not replacing the clips is generating squeaking noise _down the road_. It doesn’t really matter if you replace your vehicle often, but it makes all the difference if you plan to enjoy the MB drive for years!) ;-)

What worked for me: to remove the plastics screws, add some pressure on them by inserting the pry tool under the bottom edge of the panel.

Then for the sides, use the thicker and bigger pry tool you own, in 4-6 inches strokes from bottom up, on each side hedge of the door panel, (until the next clip pops)…

Conclusion: I do not recommend replacing the door panel to beginners. And it requires to add an amp.

Enjoy!

Location of the "clips". It helps to pry next to them, while gently pulling toward you.

(You can see the mic black foam "ball" popping above the USB port)

**The replacement process:**

**Prying the “vent center panel”**: refer to several YouTube video (and Crutchfield instructions) that shows how to pry the central vent console. It’s not difficult but I had a hard time locating the clips and didn’t want to pull hard. See my picture with blue tape on the clip locations. Pull gently after inserting the pry tool. Easy 2/5

**Disconnect the HU connectors**. Easy 1/5. Note: to rotate the main connector locking lever, there are 2 clips to press down (picture). (2/5)

I do recommend marking the 3 connectors (USB, BU Cam, Antenna) once unplugged from Audio 15 to ease later re-assembly, as original color coding is lost… (No connector for the BT mic.. not in use)

**Dash kit.**

Mounting the Metra 99-6512 dash kit is easy (1/5). But if the kit aligns well with the “vent center panel”, the alignment with the HU itself does not (a 2mm - 1/8” gap) and some sharp edge trimming needed to fit… (3/5)

I do not recommend this kit for this specific HU. I suggest to go with the Crutchfield recommended one.

**Reconnect the HU connectors.** Easy 1/5. Follows the clear instructions sent by CF. You basically have to connect the harness they sent (with PAC in the middle) between the HU and the MB car big connector. Then the 4 connectors with provide dongles (Antenna, BU Cam, USB)

**CarPlay and USB.**

There are 2 USB port cables on the back of HU. Long enough get them in a convenient location.

One for CarPlay/Android + charging that I plugged in the original USB (adapter supplied with the kit).

The other one to connect a USB drive + charging. I cut a rectangular opening (18 x 10mm) next to other USB port. Secure with a tie wrap. Easy.

**Mic.**

The PAC interface does not support the MB stock BT mic. You need to use the provide one. (Jack 3.5 mm). I drilled a hole next to the USB port (because I didn’t want to disassemble the pillar to run a cable). Simple. Works perfectly. (picture)

**Results and conclusion:**

**Sound quality** is good. This HU provides a significant improvement and meets my project goal.

I replaced the door speakers’ woofers (and dash tweeters) with **Rockford Fosgate R165-S**($75).

Not happy with the result. I do not recommend doing this. (Refer to the 2nd article at the end for details)

**BU Cam.**

Easy to connect. Cable comes in a bag. Works perfectly. Great improvement from stock cam image quality. Requires to be in reverse gear. There is a way to turn it on from the HU “Radio Screen” while driving, which is great to check trailer, bike rack, load rack…

You will get both the “bendy lines” from the stock camera and the HU. You just need to disable the HU lines in the BU setting menu.

**HU User interface** is nice and easy to use (songs and radio information on the HU screen). All SW commands work.

**The drawbacks/lost features:**

- I miss the text for phone and audio information inside the driver info display. Both now show "----" but all other features retained. Information displayed nicely on HU screen. Including the 1000s of song on my USB key, with tiles, album cove pictures. So a none issue for me.


Root cause: The PAC role is to communicate from car to HC, but not from HU to the car. No solution.
- Lost use of the factory BT mic. None issue for me: the provided mic works great and is easy to install.
- Listen to radio with the car key out is not possible. Bummer! (Now installing a Power Delay Switch.$15)
- The slow (14 sec!) boot up and a splash screen that requires a acknowledgement of safety instruction is super annoying. BTW this makes the BU Camera useless for me because I don’t 15 sec idle…

Fix 1: Turn off EULA in the setting mebu to remove the warning splash scree. Save 2 sec.


Fix 2: I am installing a Power Delay Switch. $15 to leave the HU powered like the Audio 15 was (you choose for how long between 1s to 999 min). then it’s instant on
- The volume knob is missing indeed. Not an issue in everyday use. Just need getting used to the convenient SW control. Including instant mute. (It’s only when you want to quickly pop up your favorite song volume..!)

**The benefits/ New features:**

- CarPlay setup is quick & super easy. Just connect the phone once in the USB port, answer 2 pairing popup questions, done. Allow to use Google Map. Split screen feature (Audio + map) is cool.
- Radio user interface is much easier than Audio 15 (Access to USB and SD car, apps..)
- DVD watching (from USB drive or AV in) Not that I will use it, but cute and good quality
- Sound quality is significant improved and exactly what I expected. (13 band equalizer and booster help)
- Assembly was easier than expected. Everything works first time. ½ day job for a slow worker.

**\*Sound quality:** For me: allowing to listen at 50% of max volume with a rich sound and low distortion.

I found very little reviews about the difference in sound distortion between brands and units. And I found couple of articles saying why there is little difference between brands on this criteria for the ~20ish Watt HU (w/o external amplifier)… because they are all using the same chips… “ _the 4 channel audio amplifier chips in all head units are mostly made by ST or Hitachi. The ST chip is slightly cleaner, but it is not a significant difference. Most of the brands use both chips, so you can’t really say brand X is cleaner than brand Y”_

**Abbreviations used:** HU Head unit, MB Mercedes Benz, CarPlay Apple CarPlay, Android for Android Audio, SW Steering Wheel, 1/5 is the difficulty rating out of 5, WI wiring interface/PAC 4.2, BT Bluetooth, CF Crutchfield, Cam camera, BU Cam Backup Camera, Amp amplifier, Metris Vito,

**Issues, troubleshooting and fixes:**

I encounter couple of issues on the way.

USB thumb drive not recognized. The USB drives need to be formatted as FAT32. (Ex FAT format is not read)

Radio does not work with key out of ignition. Lost the RAP (Retain Accessory Power). MB vehicle provides RAP that keeps voltage applied to certain vehicle systems for a given time after the ignition is off and while all (or driver) doors are closed. According to Crutchfield the PAC retains the RAP. Crutchfield replaced the PAC. Didn't solve it.

The DIP Switch setting (red) on the PAC was different on the replacement unit: From Boss Audio tech support, the setting for Boss Audio is switch #3 on. (Like _other brand_)

**Conclusion:**

Replacing the HU worth it for me. Replacing the speaker without adding an amp doesn’t (for me).

I hope you enjoyed this post!. I was desperately looking for a comprehensive simple affordable solution like this and I hope it will help beginners like me to jump in this intimidating job and provide them an easy kick start. There are plenty of other posts with technical info and expert debates. Intimidating to me. Feel free to send me question in PM. I am sure there are mistakes to correct, but before you jump the gun, will it help a beginner or discourage and intimidate them? 😉(And before you shoot: please remember this is for everyone's enjoyment! 😉)

End of article about the HU replacement

Serge,

2017 Metris commercial with a 2018 camper conversion with a Reimo Pop-top roof. Love it! ;-)

**_**Dec 2nd 2024: Update after 6 months of use**_**

After 6 month of use and couple of road trips: I confirm the slight improvement in sound provided by the HU replacement, specifically at low speed and city drive. But without replacing the stock speakers, the sound lacks bass support to listen to any quality music above 60 mph.

Bottom line, replacing the HU alone doesn't worth the amount of work! (Replacing the speakers and adding an amplifier is a must to me). Just my opinion after a longer use...

\-\-\---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**More information about the speaker’s replacement.**

**I also intended to replace the MB stock speakers to get a significant sound improvement.**

Upon Crutchfield advise I purchased **Rockford Fosgate R165-S speakers.** 6 1/2” ($75) 92dB sensitivity/efficiency for 60-20k Hz. (92 dB is _noise_ generated for 1 W…92 is very good, higher: you lose too much bass)

FYI the **Kicker 46CSC674,** 6 1/2", 100w ($79) are better quality but were on back order for 2 months.

**Result:** the sound is bad and way worse than the stock speakers. The main issue is there is no bass.

Disclaimer: I don’t know anything about car stereo, but I studied acoustic in college and I spent a few hours researching on line and with experts during project.

First you need to choose between Component speakers or wide range speakers:

Component speakers means you have several speakers to cover the auditive spectrum (tweeter, woofer, subwoofer), each covering a range of frequencies. vs a wide range covering the whole spectrum (often with several coaxial speakers inside).

Second, you need enough power to “move” the speaker!

20 to 25W x 4 channels from the HU is **not** enough power and mover the 90ish dB speakers at low frequency, hence the better sound from the MB stock speakers.

(An expert will argue that it’s still "objectively a better sound quality", but who cares if you have no bass to listen? I am not starting an expert technical debate for the sake of it, just looking for a common sense solution for listening enjoyment)

The MB stock paper light speakers actually “simulates” the bass. And if you turn on the “Loudness surround” feature in the Boss HU settings (signal compression) you get very decent bass sound.

The MB speakers also have significant higher diameter (40% more area…). They sound very good until halfway of the volume. (Not super load indeed but plenty enough for me)

The next step for sound improvement is to add an amplifier to move the speakers. There is ample empty space right below the HU, inside the dash (see YouTube video). Cheapest solutions:

- Kenwood: KAC-M1814 $169. 4x 45W. Bridging the 2 channels gives 2x 90 W
- Pioneer 60 W x 2 . $89.

But you will have to run 12V to the amp and speaker wires to the speakers. And that’s beyond my limits!

(and yes that was a frustrating experience that the CF sales person did not tell me this issue when you purchase. The tech support guy knew it and they "have the same training")

Last, **prying the “vent center panel” for the HU was very easy in comparison of prying the door panels.**

If you attempt it with no experience: watch a few videos first.

I found the prying and putting back the door panels difficult (4/5).

One issue is that some of the 9 orange clips popped from the door panel rather that popping out of the door.

Another is none of the 3 bottom Philips screws came out (they are plastic screws). So you break the plastic snaps when prying. (see pictures)

(Some people would say “who cares!?” My experience with damaging and not replacing the clips is generating squeaking noise _down the road_. It doesn’t really matter if you replace your vehicle often, but it makes all the difference if you plan to enjoy the MB drive for years!) ;-)

What worked for me: to remove the plastics screws, add some pressure on them by inserting the pry tool under the bottom edge of the panel.

Then for the sides, use the thicker and bigger pry tool you own, in 4-6 inches strokes from bottom up, on each side hedge of the door panel, (until the next clip pops)…

Conclusion: I do not recommend replacing the door panel to beginners. And it requires to add an amp.

Enjoy!
